issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed of the expanded state of the navigation menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in the close function, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the expanded state of dropdown menus, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter updates the visible card count and grid without announcing the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated results, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after updating the count, add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates all price displays without announcing the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the price updates, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after updating the cards, add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In init, after setting _trigger, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In init, after registering each dropdown, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel slide change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slide changes are not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new slide, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In goTo, after updating the slide, add liveRegion.announce(`Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}: ${_slides[target].label}`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In open, add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in close, add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion selection missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Selecting a search suggestion does not announce the action via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the selection, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In _selectSuggestion, after selecting the item, add liveRegion.announce(`Selected ${item.label}`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter animation missing aria-hidden update
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter animation hides cards without updating aria-hidden. Screen reader users may still perceive hidden cards, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In run, when hiding cards, add setAttr(el, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); when showing, removeAttr(el, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility missing aria-hidden update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden/shown without updating aria-hidden. Screen reader users may still perceive the hidden drawer, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In open, removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN); in close, setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility missing aria-hidden update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden/shown without updating aria-hidden. Screen reader users may still perceive hidden panels, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN); in _closeInstance, setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing keyboard handler for non-button element
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch is a div with role="switch" but lacks a keydown handler for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle with standard keys, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: In init, add _toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel auto-play missing user control
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-plays without a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism that persists across page loads. Users with cognitive disabilities may be disrupted, violating WCAG 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: In init, store the auto-play preference in localStorage and respect it on load; provide a persistent pause button.

issuesFound.title: form validation missing aria-invalid clearance on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator does not clear aria-invalid when an input becomes valid. Screen reader users may continue to hear invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when valid, add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); when invalid, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion search filter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Filtering FAQ items by search query does not announce the result count via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the filtered results, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In _filterByQuery, after filtering, add liveRegion.announce(`${matching.length} question${matching.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Clearing the search input does not announce the action via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the search has been cleared, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In clear, after closing the panel, add liveRegion.announce('Search cleared');

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1164: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` functions use CSS classes to show/hide the drawer and overlay but do not update `aria-hidden`. This may leave the hidden menu accessible to screen readers while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1146: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`, Line 1162: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader._onScroll` function toggles the visibility of the header (hiding it on scroll-down), but this state change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1458: `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden');` (after adding `CSS.headerHidden`)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function cycles the auto-play state, but the `_playBtn` does not have its `aria-pressed` attribute updated to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2138: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing ? 'true' : 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2387: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function handles the valid state by removing the error class, but it does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2382: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons track an active state via `activeFilter === tab.value`, but do not use `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected state to screen readers.

issuesFound.fix: Line 840: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (mobile nav toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle sets min‑height and min‑width to 28 px, below the 44 px target size for touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (top‑level nav links/buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: #nav‑menu > li > a, #nav‑menu > li > button have min‑height: 28 px, which is too small for finger tapping.
issuesFound.fix: Line 936: #nav‑menu > li > a, #nav‑menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (filter tab buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: .filter-tabs button sets min‑height: 28 px, below the recommended 44 px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: .filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (pricing toggle buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-toggle button sets min‑height: 28 px, which does not meet the 44 px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1505: .pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (social‑link anchors)
issuesFound.explanation: .social-links a sets min‑height: 28 px, below the required 44 px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1705: .social-links a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: body sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which can cause words to overlap and be hard to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes the visual underline from all links without providing another visual cue (e.g., color change, border), making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (hero image hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img:hover applies a transform (rotateY/rotateX) and a box‑shadow transition, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disabling this animation, violating reduced‑motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1402: .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (hero CTA button hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover uses transform: translateY(-1px) with a transition, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion override is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (pricing card hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-card:hover applies a translateY(-2px) transform with a transition, lacking a prefers‑reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (featured pricing card hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-card--featured:hover applies translateY(-4px) and scale(1.01) with a transition, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (stat item hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .stat-item:hover applies translateY(-2px) with a transition, without a prefers‑reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (product card hover)
issuesFound.explanation: .product-card:hover applies translateY(-3px) with a transition, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: .product-card:hover { transform: none; } /* Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons (e.g. `#nav-menu > li > button[aria-haspopup]`) are never updated with `aria‑expanded="true"` or `"false"` when the panel opens or closes. Screen‑reader users cannot know the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` on open and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` on close.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the dropdown triggers have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving the default state ambiguous for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.register` after creating the trigger, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons (`[data-faq-trigger]`) never receive `aria‑expanded` changes when an item is opened or closed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `faqAccordion.close` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are rendered without an initial `aria‑expanded` value on their triggers, making the default state unclear.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse` after ensuring ARIA attributes, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) acts as an on/off switch but never updates `aria‑pressed`, violating ARIA button‑state expectations.
issuesFound.fix: In `pricing._onToggleClick` (or the click handler) add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))` and update it on each period change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria‑invalid="true"`; assistive technologies therefore do not announce errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` when `!result.valid`, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `aria‑invalid` is never removed, leaving stale error state for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` when `result.valid`, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count (`[data-product-count]`) is updated, but no ARIA live region announces the new total, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge in `productFilter._updateCountBadge`, also set `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`)` or update a hidden live region element.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer visibility toggles do not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer opens/closes, the drawer element’s visibility is changed via CSS classes only; `aria‑hidden` is never set, so AT may still treat it as visible when closed.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false')`; in `mobileNav.close` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The header is hidden on scroll by adding/removing a CSS class, but its `aria‑hidden` state is never toggled, leaving assistive tech unaware of its visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In `stickyHeader._onScroll` when `_hidden` becomes true, call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when it becomes false, remove the attribute.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion missing initial aria‑expanded
Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid inputs
Sticky header hide/show does not update aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not clear aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel but never resets `aria‑expanded` to “false”. Screen readers therefore think the menu remains open, causing confusion. This is also covered by sweep JS‑A.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2213: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the hamburger button (`_trigger`) is wired for opening/closing but its `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “false” on page load. Users of assistive tech cannot know the menu’s collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1806: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer not hidden from AT when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` removes CSS classes and scroll‑lock but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element (`_drawer`). The drawer remains perceivable to screen readers even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1848: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`  
    Line 1850 (in `mobileNav.open()`): `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: `_renderResult()` in `inlineValidator` adds/removes error messages but never toggles the `aria‑invalid` attribute on the field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect that the field is invalid. This violates the “Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid” sweep.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5608 (invalid branch): `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`  
    Line 5612 (valid branch): `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button selected by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is made focusable and clickable but never receives an `aria‑label` or visible text, leaving it without an accessible name. This fails the “Missing accessible name” rule for `<a>`/`<button>`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2506: `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll back to top');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing initial aria‑expanded value (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “false”. AT users cannot know the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close routine does not remove aria‑expanded (true) (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel but leaves `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger, causing a mismatch between visual and accessibility states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2214: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` changes the numeric badge but does not announce the new count via a live region, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the filtered result count. This violates the “Dynamic content updates not announced via live region” sweep.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2992 (after setting text): `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} product\${count===1?'':'s'} shown\`);`

fpTitles: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdownMenu module adds/removes the “hidden” attribute on the panel but never calls `setAttribute('aria-expanded', …)` on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing initial aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `register` the dropdown trigger receives `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving its state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions call `setExpanded(trigger, …)` which updates the panel but the trigger element (`_trigger`) never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value, so its expanded state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In `setExpanded` (or directly in `mobileNav.open/close`) set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` on open and `false` on close.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) toggles the drawer but never updates `aria-pressed`, a required state indicator for a toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: After opening set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; after closing set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger is not given an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer (`SELECTORS.navMenu`), breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger` and `_drawer`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel hidden via `hidden` but not `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The generic helper `setExpanded` hides the panel with `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: When `expanded` is false, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when true, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden via `hidden` but not `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: In `_closeInstance` the dropdown panel is hidden with `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`, so assistive technologies may still read it.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input lacks `aria-invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.bind` is used on the newsletter email field but the validator only adds error messages; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input when the value is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult`, when `!result.valid` also call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “First name” input lacks `aria-invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The first‑name field is bound with `inlineValidator.bind` but the validator does not set `aria-invalid`, so screen‑reader users are not notified of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 8 – ensure `aria-invalid="true"` is set on error and cleared on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email” input lacks `aria-invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 9 for the email field; validation does not reflect the error via `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 8.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the text of the element with `data-product-count` but no ARIA live region is associated, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden live region (e.g., `<span aria-live="polite" class="sr-only">`) linked via `aria-describedby` to the count badge and update its text when the count changes.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not announce state changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions emit custom events but never update an ARIA live region, so screen‑reader users do not hear that a question was expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a live region (e.g., `
`) and, in `open`, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`)`; in `close`, announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing `aria-controls` reference to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: Although `register` sets `aria-controls` when a panel ID exists, if the panel lacks an `id` the attribute is not added, breaking the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every dropdown panel receives a unique `id` (e.g., via `srUtils.ensureId(panel)`) before setting `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience nausea or dizziness from the movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform that creates a 3D rotation effect on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1055 is empty and does not disable this animation. Users with motion sensitivity may be adversely affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px as a replacement. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring when outline-width is zero. Keyboard users will not see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 322: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector removes the default focus outline for mouse users (via :focus:not(:focus-visible)) without providing a visible alternative. While this specifically targets non-keyboard focus, it removes the fallback outline entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 328: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--colour-border); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users without providing a forced-colors alternative like a thick border.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334-336: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, and provides no box-shadow, border, or alternative visual indicator to show keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433-436: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible to sighted users, breaking the visual layout and potentially exposing content that should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349-358: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transition: transform and transform effects on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 542 is empty and does not disable the transform transition or hover effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 542-545: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards include transform in their transition and apply transform: translateY(-3px) on hover, but lack a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform for its slide animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this movement for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 385: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 519: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226: `word-spacing: normal;` or `word-spacing: 0;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses white text (#ffffff) on a blue background (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.8:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (16px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: `color: #ffffff; background-color: #004aaa;` (or darker blue meeting 4.5:1 ratio)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) to distinguish links from surrounding text at rest, causing links to rely on colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }` or provide specific underline rules for body-text links.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes via the open() and close() functions, but neither updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, leaving blind users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: Add after `_open = true;` → `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 1855: Add after `_open = false;` → `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen readers from announcing the disclosure state change to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1980: Add after `inst.open = true;` → `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 1995: Add after `inst.open = false;` → `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in faqAccordion adds CSS classes and reveals the panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the missing update in open() leaves the attribute stale when an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2285: Add after `item.open = true;` → `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in siteSearch toggle the suggestion panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the search input combobox. Screen readers cannot convey whether the suggestion list is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1650: Add after `_searchState.open = true;` → `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 1668: Add after `_searchState.open = false;` → `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey validation errors to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: Add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` → `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description association but does not clear aria-invalid, leaving the field marked as invalid to screen readers even after it becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1362: Add after the existing logic in markValid → `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The register function sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup on the trigger but omits initializing aria-expanded="false", leaving the initial collapsed state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: Add after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` → `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The init function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false", preventing screen readers from knowing the suggestion panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: Add after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` → `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and count badge but provides no aria-live announcement, so screen reader users are not informed when filter results change or how many products are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: Add after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` → `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates price displays across all plan cards but does not announce the change via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that prices have switched between monthly and annual values.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: Add after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` → `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${period} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions emit custom events but do not announce the expanded or collapsed state to screen readers via the live region, leaving users uncertain whether their action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2285: Add before closing brace of open() → `liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.question} expanded\`);`
  Line 2305: Add before closing brace of close() → `liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.question} collapsed\`);`

issuesFound.title: toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause
issuesFound.explanation: The show function in toastManager sets a timer to auto-dismiss notifications after a delay but provides no mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the timer on hover or focus, violating WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the content.

issuesFound.title: contactForm fields missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds validation to the full_name and email fields but does not set autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers and assistive technologies from programmatically identifying the purpose of these personal data inputs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2520: Add after `if (field) inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);` for full_name → `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line 2521: Add after `if (field) inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);` for email → `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds validation to the email field but does not set autocomplete="email", failing to identify the input purpose for personal data collection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Add after `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']);` → `_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard fields missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard binds validation to step fields including wizard_name and wizard_email but omits autocomplete attributes for these personal data inputs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in open(): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in close(): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" when opened or "false" when closed. Assistive technologies cannot track the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _closeInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox. The functions open and close the listbox but never update aria-expanded on the input element, leaving screen readers unaware of the listbox visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _open: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _close: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Although the close() function correctly sets it to "false", the asymmetric update means screen readers do not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Validation failure marks fields with CSS classes and links error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Valid state clearing also does not remove aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in markInvalid: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line in markValid: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is queried and stored in _trigger, but its initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" when the page loads with the menu closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line after _trigger assignment: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is registered, the panel is initially hidden, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line after setting aria-controls: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-haspopup="listbox", but the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" before the user interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line after setting aria-haspopup: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but aria-hidden="true" is not applied. When opened, aria-hidden is not removed. This can cause inconsistent accessibility tree states.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in close(): `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line in open(): `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the navigation menu is a significant context change. The functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change via the liveRegion announcer, so screen reader users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in open(): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`
  Line in close(): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus opening and closing change the available content. These functions do not announce the state change via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that a dropdown has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');`
  Line in _closeInstance: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');`

  Line for email field binding: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

  Line for wizard_email field binding: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus is not moved into the drawer. Focus remains on the trigger button, preventing keyboard users from accessing the menu content without tabbing through the entire page.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open() after _lockScroll(): `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button. Focus is lost to the document body, forcing keyboard users to start tabbing from the beginning of the page.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets item.open = true and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that content is now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqAccordion.open(), after "item.open = true;":
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox opens the suggestion panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the input element. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
In siteSearch._open(), after "_searchState.open = true;":
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the search suggestions, the function resets state variables and hides the panel, but leaves aria-expanded in its previous state. It must be set to "false" or removed to reflect the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
In siteSearch._close(), after "_searchState.open = false;":
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer sets internal state and CSS classes, but does not update aria-expanded on the trigger button to "true", preventing screen readers from announcing the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open(), after "_open = true;":
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation updates internal state but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close(), after "_open = false;":
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu panel sets inst.open = true and removes the hidden attribute, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, failing to communicate the visible state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In dropdownMenu._openInstance(), after "inst.open = true;":
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the dropdown menu removes CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree in an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix:
In dropdownMenu._closeInstance(), after "inst.open = false;":
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons cycle between active and inactive states, but the code only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state, so screen readers cannot identify which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix:
In pricing._syncToggleState(), after "toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);":
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The validation function updates visual styling and aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid on the field. Screen readers cannot convey whether the input contains invalid data.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created in a closed state, but aria-expanded="false" is never initialized on page load. Screen readers will not recognize the button as controlling expandable content.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.init(), after "_initialised = true;":
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", even though the panel is hidden. This violates the requirement that expandable triggers declare their initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
In dropdownMenu.register(), after "panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');":
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input starts with the suggestion panel closed, but aria-expanded="false" is never set on the input during initialization, leaving the accessibility tree incomplete.
issuesFound.fix:
In siteSearch.init(), after "const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);":
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer closes, it is hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree. If CSS uses off-screen positioning rather than display:none, screen readers can still access the hidden content. The code must set aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close(), after "removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);":
setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-hidden removal on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the drawer, if it was previously hidden with aria-hidden="true", the attribute must be removed or set to "false" to make the content accessible. The code only manages CSS classes.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open(), after "addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);":
removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible content updates (cards shown/hidden, count badge updates), but no aria-live region announces the result count or filter change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In productFilter.applyFilters(), inside the filterAnimation.run callback after "_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);":
liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found for current filter`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods dynamically updates price text content, but screen readers are not notified of the change via a live region, so users may not perceive the updated prices.
issuesFound.fix:
In pricing.setPeriod(), after the forEach loop updating plan cards:
liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure trigger button in NavDropdown manages an isOpen state and renders a sub-menu, but lacks aria-expanded. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the sub-menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggling mobile navigation sub-sections (Solutions, Resources) manages isExpanded state but does not expose it via aria-expanded, leaving assistive technology unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button toggles the answer panel visibility using isOpen state but omits aria-expanded, preventing screen-reader users from knowing if the panel is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile hamburger toggle button controls the visibility of the navigation panel (isOpen state) but only changes its aria-label text; it lacks aria-expanded, so the state change is not communicated programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1316: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
